steps.title: Use the definition
steps.content: The text defines autofiction as fiction that emphasizes autobiographical elements. It says authors pattern protagonists' lives after their own and often use first-person, unusually direct, almost confessional narration. Supporting the description of Vuong's novel means finding evidence that the protagonist resembles Vuong and that the narrative voice has that frank quality.

steps.title: Separate the evidence
steps.content: Choice B discusses chronology and letter form, but those details do not show autobiographical life-patterning or a confessional voice. Choice C discusses poetic imagery, which blurs poetry and fiction rather than author and character. Choice D gives resemblance to Vuong, but the protagonist's hidden real name weakens the specific naming convention mentioned in the text. Choice A combines a Vuong-like family background with surprisingly frank narration, matching the two defining features. The answer is A.
